A Global Time Story

AMor PM?

Only 12 nations on Earth officially use the 12-hour clock. Here's who they are — and why it matters.

The Story Begins

A World Divided by Time Format

Most of the world reads time on a 24-hour clock. Yet a handful of nations — shaped largely by British colonial history — still phrase time in two 12-hour halves, requiring the AM/PM suffix to avoid confusion.

Scroll through each country to discover its population, story, and place on the globe.

12-Hour Time countries
24-Hour Time countries
The Common Thread

Nearly every 12-hour nation shares a history of British colonial influence — from India to Australia, Canada to South Africa. The AM/PM system traveled with the Empire.

~3.5B
People still saying AM & PM every day
